rulings.Case Title: TATIANA SANOCHKINA et al VS. MATHEW HUI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Appoint Attorney Or To Appear In Person And Notice Of Motion Adn Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Or , In The Alernative, Discretionary Dismissal Of Pltf'S Complaint, And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 18, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MABEL TENG'S Motion To Appoint Attorney Or To Appear In Person And Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Or, In The Alernative, Discretionary Dismissal Of Plaintiff's Complaint, And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. The motion is denied. There are no grounds for a mandatory or discretionary dismissal because this action was stayed from October 11, 2007 (order granting stay) to February 4, 2013 (remittitur issued). The stay tolled the running of the statutory time limitations. See CCP 583.240(b). The arbitration proceedings were resolved only after issuance of the remittitur. Federal Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 1370, 1374-1375. The motion to quash is denied as moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing To Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 18, 2014, Line 4, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award. HEARING REQUIRED. Judge Miller discloses and will disclose at the hearing that she knows and is social friends with Deputy City Attorney Elizabeth Salveson. Judge Miller practiced law in the same firm as Ms. Salveson from December 1981 - March 1987, and from 1992 through 1995. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case, and she does not believe that a person aware of these facts would reasonably have a doubt that she would be able to be impartial. =(302/MJM)
